name: World Entertainment
description: Movies, TV shows, gaming, and pop culture from around the world — lights, camera, trivia!
author: Game Night Crew

questions.question: In 'The Lion King', what is the name of Simba's father?
questions.explanation: Mufasa is the OG king of Pride Rock. That stampede scene still hits different — even on your fifth rewatch at 2am.

questions.question: Which TV show features the fictional country of Westeros?
questions.explanation: Game of Thrones brought Westeros to life. Everyone had a favourite house — and if you say House Bolton, we need to talk.

questions.question: What is the name of the plumber who rescues Princess Peach in Nintendo games?
questions.explanation: It's-a-me, Mario! The most famous plumber in history — he's saved more princesses than he's fixed pipes.

questions.question: Which movie franchise features a character named Jack Sparrow?
questions.explanation: Captain Jack Sparrow, if you please! Johnny Depp's wobbly pirate became one of the most iconic characters in cinema history.

questions.question: Which animated movie features a clownfish searching for his son?
questions.explanation: Finding Nemo! Marlin crossed the entire ocean for his boy. That's top-tier parenting right there.

questions.question: In the TV show 'Friends', what is the name of the coffee shop where the group hangs out?
questions.explanation: Central Perk — where six New Yorkers somehow always had the best couch reserved. Try that at a Vida e in Cape Town, good luck!

questions.question: What video game series features a battle royale mode called 'Warzone'?
questions.explanation: Call of Duty: Warzone had the whole world dropping into Verdansk. SA gamers were locked in too, load-shedding permitting!

questions.question: Which streaming platform produced the Korean series 'Squid Game'?
questions.explanation: Netflix dropped Squid Game in 2021 and it became the most-watched series in the platform's history. Red light, green light went from playground to nightmare!

questions.question: Who directed the movie 'Titanic' (1997)?
questions.explanation: James Cameron directed Titanic and told the world 'I'm the king of the world!' at the Oscars. The man does NOT do things by halves.

questions.question: In 'Breaking Bad', what subject does Walter White teach before becoming a drug manufacturer?
questions.explanation: Chemistry, obviously! Walt went from marking homework to cooking blue meth. That's one heck of a career pivot.

questions.question: Arrange these Disney animated films from EARLIEST to LATEST release.
options: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs
questions.explanation: Snow White (1937), The Little Mermaid (1989), The Lion King (1994), Frozen (2013). Disney's been at it for nearly a century — that's commitment!

questions.question: Which actor played the Joker in 'The Dark Knight' (2008)?
questions.explanation: Heath Ledger's Joker performance was legendary and earned him a posthumous Oscar. 'Why so serious?' became instant pop culture gold.

questions.question: What is the highest-grossing film of all time (not adjusted for inflation)?
questions.explanation: Avatar (2009) reclaimed the top spot after its re-releases, sitting at around $2.9 billion. James Cameron just keeps winning.

questions.question: Which Japanese animation studio created 'Spirited Away' and 'My Neighbor Totoro'?
questions.explanation: Studio Ghibli, founded by the legendary Hayao Miyazaki. Spirited Away even won the Oscar for Best Animated Feature in 2003!

questions.question: Which comedian is known for specials like 'Afraid of the Dark' and was born in Johannesburg, South Africa?
questions.explanation: Trevor Noah — born and raised in Mzansi! He went from Soweto to hosting The Daily Show. Proudly South African, that one.

questions.question: In the video game 'Minecraft', what is the final boss called?
questions.explanation: The Ender Dragon lives in The End dimension. Millions of kids (and let's be honest, adults too) have made that portal journey!

questions.question: Which TV series had an episode called 'The Red Wedding' that shocked viewers worldwide?
questions.explanation: Game of Thrones, Season 3. The Red Wedding destroyed us all. Nobody was safe in Westeros — George R.R. Martin showed no mercy!

questions.question: Which Pixar movie features the emotion characters Joy, Sadness, Anger, Fear, and Disgust?
questions.explanation: Inside Out (2015) turned emotions into characters and made grown adults cry — which is ironic when you think about it.

questions.question: What is the name of the fictional African nation in the Marvel Cinematic Universe known for its vibranium?
questions.explanation: Wakanda forever! Black Panther put a fictional African superpower on the map. SA vibes were strong in that movie — some scenes were even filmed in our backyard!

questions.question: Which video game franchise features characters named Master Chief and Cortana?
questions.explanation: Halo! Master Chief is Xbox's poster boy. And yes, Microsoft named their AI assistant Cortana after the game character.

questions.question: Arrange these Marvel Cinematic Universe films in order of release, EARLIEST to LATEST.
questions.explanation: Iron Man (2008), The Avengers (2012), Black Panther (2018), Avengers: Endgame (2019). The MCU built up to that Endgame moment for over a decade!

questions.question: Which Broadway musical features the songs 'Defying Gravity' and 'Popular'?
questions.explanation: Wicked tells the untold story of the witches of Oz. The 2024 movie adaptation finally brought it to the big screen after years of waiting!

questions.question: Which country's film industry is commonly known as 'Bollywood'?
questions.explanation: Bollywood is India's Hindi-language film industry based in Mumbai. It produces more films per year than Hollywood — with way more dancing!

questions.question: In the anime series 'Dragon Ball Z', what is Goku's Saiyan name?
questions.explanation: Kakarot! Every SA kid who grew up watching DBZ on SABC 2 remembers Vegeta screaming that name. Good times.

questions.question: Which film won the Oscar for Best Picture in 2020, becoming the first non-English language film to do so?
questions.explanation: Parasite from South Korea swept the Oscars! Director Bong Joon-ho made history. Korean entertainment was already taking over the world.

questions.question: Which actress has won the most Academy Awards for acting?
questions.explanation: Katharine Hepburn won four Best Actress Oscars. Meryl Streep has three (plus the most nominations ever), but Hepburn still holds the record!

questions.question: In 'The Witcher' video game series, what is Geralt of Rivia's profession?
questions.explanation: Geralt is a Witcher — a mutant monster hunter for hire. The games, books, and Netflix series all became massive hits worldwide.

questions.question: Which stand-up comedian is known for the Netflix special 'Sticks & Stones' and the sketch show 'Chappelle's Show'?
questions.explanation: Dave Chappelle — one of the GOATs of stand-up comedy. Chappelle's Show was groundbreaking, and he walked away from $50 million. Legend move!

questions.question: Which Nigerian film industry is the third-largest in the world by number of films produced?
questions.explanation: Nollywood is massive! Nigeria produces thousands of films each year. African cinema is booming, and Nollywood leads the way on the continent.

questions.question: Which director is known for mind-bending films like 'Inception', 'Interstellar', and 'Tenet'?
questions.explanation: Christopher Nolan loves making you watch a movie three times before you actually understand it. No complaints though — the man is a genius!

questions.question: Arrange these streaming platforms by their global launch year, EARLIEST to LATEST.
questions.explanation: Netflix streaming (2007), Amazon Prime Video (2011), Disney+ (Nov 2019), Apple TV+ (Nov 2019 — same month, but Disney+ launched days earlier). The streaming wars are real!

questions.question: What was the first feature-length film to be entirely computer-animated?
questions.explanation: Toy Story (1995) by Pixar was the first fully computer-animated feature film. It changed animation forever — to infinity and beyond!

questions.question: In the Harry Potter films, who played Professor Severus Snape?
questions.explanation: Alan Rickman's portrayal of Snape is iconic. 'Always.' — if that one word doesn't make you emotional, are you even human?

questions.question: Which video game holds the record for most copies sold of a single game (as of 2024)?
questions.explanation: Minecraft has sold over 300 million copies across all platforms. It's the best-selling video game ever — and people are STILL playing it!

questions.question: Which South African-born filmmaker directed 'District 9' (2009), a sci-fi film set in Johannesburg?
questions.explanation: Neill Blomkamp put Joburg on the Hollywood map with District 9! Aliens landing in SA — only a local could make that story work so well.

questions.question: Which TV series created by Vince Gilligan is a prequel to 'Breaking Bad'?
questions.explanation: Better Call Saul tells the origin story of lawyer Jimmy McGill becoming Saul Goodman. Six seasons of absolute quality television — some say it's even better than Breaking Bad!

questions.question: Which anime series follows a boy named Monkey D. Luffy searching for the 'One Piece' treasure?
questions.explanation: One Piece has been running since 1999 and is one of the best-selling manga series of all time. Luffy's journey just keeps going — over 1,100 episodes!

questions.question: Which film won the first-ever Academy Award for Best Picture in 1929?
options: Sunrise: A Song of Two Humans
All Quiet on the Western Front
questions.explanation: Wings (1927) won Best Picture at the very first Oscars ceremony. It's a silent World War I film — before talkies even took over!

questions.question: Which Broadway musical tells the story of American founding father Alexander Hamilton using hip-hop music?
questions.explanation: Hamilton by Lin-Manuel Miranda revolutionised Broadway. History class meets hip-hop — and it actually works. The Disney+ recording brought it to the world!

questions.question: Which Korean director made the 2003 film 'Oldboy', considered a masterpiece of revenge cinema?
questions.explanation: Park Chan-wook directed Oldboy as part of his Vengeance Trilogy. Korean cinema has been producing world-class films for decades — Hollywood just took a while to notice!

questions.question: Arrange these 'Grand Theft Auto' games in order of release, EARLIEST to LATEST.
questions.explanation: Vice City (2002), San Andreas (2004), GTA IV (2008), GTA V (2013). And somehow GTA V is STILL getting re-released on every new console!

questions.question: Which director's filmography includes 'Pulp Fiction', 'Kill Bill', and 'Django Unchained'?
questions.explanation: Quentin Tarantino — the king of non-linear storytelling and sharp dialogue. He says he'll retire after his tenth film. We'll see about that!

questions.question: Which video game studio developed 'The Last of Us' and the 'Uncharted' series?
questions.explanation: Naughty Dog is a PlayStation-exclusive studio known for incredible storytelling. The Last of Us even became a hit HBO series!

questions.question: Which comedian holds the record for the most-watched comedy special on Netflix with 'He's All That' (2023)?
questions.explanation: Matt Rife's 'Natural Selection' (2023) broke Netflix comedy special records. He went from TikTok fame to global stand-up stardom practically overnight!

questions.question: Which Studio Ghibli film features a moving castle controlled by a wizard named Howl?
questions.explanation: Howl's Moving Castle (2004) is a Miyazaki classic. Between the animation, the story, and that fire demon Calcifer — it's pure magic.

questions.question: Which long-running musical holds the record for the most performances on Broadway before closing in 2023?
questions.explanation: The Phantom of the Opera ran for 35 years on Broadway (1988-2023) with over 13,900 performances. That chandelier dropped more times than your phone!

questions.question: Which Indian filmmaker directed the critically acclaimed 'Pather Panchali' (1955), considered a masterpiece of world cinema?
questions.explanation: Satyajit Ray's Apu Trilogy beginning with Pather Panchali is considered one of the greatest achievements in cinema history. He received an honorary Oscar in 1992.

questions.question: Which video game, released in 1993, is credited with popularising the first-person shooter genre?
questions.explanation: Doom (1993) by id Software is credited with popularising the FPS genre. Wolfenstein 3D came first (1992), but Doom made it mainstream and created a cultural phenomenon!

questions.question: Which actor holds the record for most Oscar nominations without a win as of 2024, with eight nominations?
questions.explanation: Glenn Close has been nominated eight times and never won. Peter O'Toole had eight too but received an honorary Oscar. Hollywood owes Glenn Close, honestly!

questions.question: Which 1927 German Expressionist film by Fritz Lang depicted a dystopian future city and is considered one of the most influential sci-fi films ever made?
questions.explanation: Metropolis (1927) by Fritz Lang was way ahead of its time. The visuals influenced everything from Blade Runner to music videos. Nearly 100 years old and still iconic!

questions.question: Which superhero is also known as 'The Man of Steel'?
questions.explanation: Superman, aka Clark Kent, aka Kal-El. The OG superhero, flying around in a cape since 1938. Not to be confused with Iron Man — that one's just a rich guy in a suit.

questions.question: Which fantasy film trilogy is based on J.R.R. Tolkien's novels and directed by Peter Jackson?
questions.explanation: The Lord of the Rings trilogy won 17 Oscars across all three films. Return of the King alone won 11 — tying the record for most wins in a single year. Absolute cinematic masterclass.

questions.question: Which streaming service created the hit series 'Stranger Things'?
questions.explanation: Stranger Things debuted on Netflix in 2016 and became one of the most-watched shows in streaming history. If you haven't watched it yet... what have you been doing?

questions.question: Which actress played Katniss Everdeen in 'The Hunger Games' films?
questions.explanation: Jennifer Lawrence was just 20 years old when she was cast as Katniss. She went on to win an Oscar for 'Silver Linings Playbook' the same year. Busy woman.

questions.question: The video game 'The Legend of Zelda' is made by which company?
questions.explanation: Nintendo has been making Zelda games since 1986. Fun fact: you play as Link, not Zelda. Zelda is the princess. Every gamer has had this argument at least once.

questions.question: Which animated TV show has been on the air since 1989, making it the longest-running American animated series?
questions.explanation: The Simpsons has been going since 1989 — over 35 seasons! At this point, the show has been on longer than many of its viewers have been alive.

questions.question: Which film was the first to use CGI (computer-generated imagery) for a main character?
questions.explanation: Tron (1982) was the first film to use extensive CGI, featuring computer-generated environments and characters. Jurassic Park (1993) later raised the bar with photorealistic dinosaurs. Both were groundbreaking.

questions.question: Arrange these video game consoles in order of release, EARLIEST to LATEST: (1) PlayStation 2 (2) Nintendo 64 (3) Xbox 360 (4) Nintendo Wii
questions.explanation: N64 (1996), PS2 (2000), Xbox 360 (2005), Wii (2006). The PS2 is still the best-selling console ever with over 155 million units sold. Absolute legend.

questions.question: Which TV series features a chemistry teacher who turns to manufacturing methamphetamine and has a spin-off called 'Better Call Saul'?
questions.explanation: Breaking Bad (2008-2013) is widely considered one of the greatest TV dramas ever made. Better Call Saul (2015-2022) is its prequel. Both are absolutely brilliant.

questions.question: In the Marvel Cinematic Universe, what is the name of Thanos's home planet?
questions.explanation: Thanos is from Titan, one of Saturn's moons in the MCU. He's called the 'Mad Titan' for a reason. Collecting infinity stones like they're Pokémon cards.

questions.question: Which acclaimed TV series set in the 1960s advertising world stars Jon Hamm as Don Draper?
questions.explanation: Mad Men ran from 2007-2015 and won 16 Emmy Awards. It's basically a masterclass in how to dress sharp, drink whiskey, and make bad life decisions.

questions.question: Which director created the sci-fi film 'Blade Runner' (1982) and later directed 'Gladiator' (2000)?
questions.explanation: Ridley Scott directed both Blade Runner and Gladiator, plus Alien. The man basically defined three different genres. Still going strong in his 80s!

questions.question: Which 2019 film by Bong Joon-ho became the first non-English language film to win the Academy Award for Best Picture?
questions.explanation: Parasite won four Oscars including Best Picture and Best Director. Bong Joon-ho's acceptance speech where he said 'I'm ready to drink tonight' was iconic.

questions.question: In the TV series 'The Wire', which fictional city is the primary setting?
questions.explanation: The Wire is set in Baltimore, Maryland and examines the city through its drug trade, ports, government, schools, and media. Often called the greatest TV series ever made. If you haven't watched it, sort that out immediately.

questions.question: Which video game series, created by Hideo Kojima, features a character named Solid Snake?
questions.explanation: Metal Gear has been running since 1987. Kojima's storytelling is legendary — the plots are so complex they make Inception look like a children's book.

questions.question: Arrange these Best Picture Oscar winners from EARLIEST to LATEST: (1) Schindler's List (2) The Godfather (3) No Country for Old Men (4) 12 Years a Slave
questions.explanation: The Godfather (1972), Schindler's List (1993), No Country for Old Men (2007), 12 Years a Slave (2013). All absolute masterpieces. If you haven't seen any of these, clear your weekend.

questions.question: Which South Korean TV drama, aired on tvN in 2016, became the highest-rated Korean drama in cable television history at the time?
questions.explanation: Goblin (Guardian: The Lonely and Great God) peaked at 20.5% viewership in South Korea. K-dramas have taken over the world — Netflix figured that out fast.

questions.question: Which 1994 film, considered one of the greatest ever made, was a box office disappointment on release but became a hit through home video rentals?
questions.explanation: The Shawshank Redemption earned only $58 million against a $25 million budget in theatres. It's now #1 on IMDb's Top 250. Goes to show — sometimes the world needs time to catch up with greatness.

questions.question: Which experimental 1929 film by Luis Buñuel and Salvador Dalí features the infamous eyeball-slicing scene?
questions.explanation: Un Chien Andalou is a 21-minute surrealist film that still shocks audiences nearly 100 years later. Dalí and Buñuel intentionally made it impossible to interpret logically. Mission accomplished.

questions.question: Which was the first commercial video game to generate $1 billion in revenue?
questions.explanation: Space Invaders (1978) by Taito generated over $1 billion in revenue by 1982 — that's about $3.8 billion adjusted for inflation. It caused a 100-yen coin shortage in Japan. The game that started an entire industry.
